transcript.clean_text: time trial time at the tour of luxembourg something of an oddity for this race which is usually decided by just a handful of seconds a long time trial of 25.4 kilometers along for this race anyway a sporting course with some rises and falls may well be that some surprises will be thrown up by non-time trial specialists mark hershey hoping that that would be the case for him harry sweeney came home just to edge tiberi's time at the line in fact sweeney's intermediate looked unbreakable for a while but then chelmos the dane decided to impose himself so much so he was finding two minute men out on course and really the timing sheets belong to him a remarkable margin well some remarkable moments as well for balcomolomer getting himself out of shape indeed a dramatic crash which remarkably he picked himself up from very very quickly not much damage thank heaven despite the drama well a warning to everybody out there but catanio was powering through the course and indeed bossing it when it came to the intermediate split he absolutely owned it what about the remainder of the course however and what about this one xiao al maida well starting off at the top of the day as favorite not only to take the stage but take control of the race as well led of course by a margin of only four seconds by mark hershey was last off the ramp for the chance of mark hershey catching almeida out there very very slim when you saw what tanya was doing out on course look at shelmo's time and look at catanius absolutely remarkable benchmark at the split and at the line david de la cruz was keeping everyone honest as you think about a podium place you can be sure of that a strong performance as well from pierre lator who spent much of his career looking after others in the high ground and now he's back to time trialling which is something he did very well as a youngster he wrestled the course and produced a top five finish on the day third at this point and that's because almeida was still out there and eating into gaps not into catania's time however at the split there was a margin between them of 8.6 seconds but how close would hershey be to almeida the man who was about to take yellow off his back well the answer was about 18 seconds at this point even further down on catanio's time de la cruz coming to the line 48.4 kilometers per hour the average for the run i'm thinking about an overall podium i am certain catania though not celebrating yet the reason was that almeida was still out on course and in attacking mode well it wasn't pretty but it was very very close are made up just missing out catania taking a famous win and having won the opening stage and now in the lead of the race well the gap was already a big one to mark hershey at the split how bad would it be at the finish line hershey tumbling away i'm afraid out of yellow a respectable time trial for a man who is not a specialist hershey finishing in sixth place ahead of jack bauer sweeney tiberi and vichenzo niebeli who produced a stunner catania takes it smiles breaking out for him and omada takes the lead of the race one stage to go and xiao almeida it is that leads by 43 seconds from mark hershey catania up into third place in de la cruz in fourth le tour rounding out your top five all positions to be decided on the final day in luxembourg you
